perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Except as set forth in Section 4.1(b) above, Inktomi shall supply to Microsoft written invoices for all amounts due under this Agreement, and payments will be due net [*] ([*]) days from Microsoft's receipt of such invoice. Inktomi shall bear sole responsibility for all expenses incurred in connection with the performance of the Services, unless otherwise set forth herein or agreed to in writing by Microsoft.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Except as set forth in Section 4.1(b) above, Inktomi shall supply to Microsoft written invoices for all amounts due under this Agreement, and payments will be due net [*] ([*]) days from Microsoft's receipt of such invoice, except that all invoices exceeding $[*] will be due immediately upon receipt. Inktomi shall bear sole responsibility for all expenses incurred in connection with the performance of the Services, unless otherwise set forth herein or agreed to in writing by Microsoft.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying Section 4.2 to state that invoices exceeding $[*] are due immediately upon receipt, contradicting the earlier stated payment term of net [*] ([*]) days for all invoices. This creates confusion as to which payment term applies to invoices exceeding $[*], leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The contradicted text is section 4.1(b) which reffers to payments, which are due in immediately available funds on the first business day of each month. Now there is an overlapping uncertainty between 4.2 and section 4.1(b).

perturbation.original_text:
10.1 Inktomi may terminate this Agreement without cause upon one year's prior written notice, provided that such notice may not be given prior to the second anniversary of the Launch Date.
10.2 Microsoft may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon [*] ([*]) days prior written notice. Upon receipt of such notice, Inktomi will discontinue all work hereunder. If Microsoft terminates this Agreement without cause pursuant to this Section 10.2, then Microsoft will pay for all services provided by Inktomi up until the date of termination under this Section 10.2.
perturbation.changed_text:
10.1 Inktomi may terminate this Agreement without cause upon one year's prior written notice, provided that such notice may not be given prior to the second anniversary of the Launch Date, unless Inktomi is acquired, in which case Inktomi may terminate this agreement immediately.
10.2 Microsoft may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon [*] ([*]) days prior written notice. Upon receipt of such notice, Inktomi will discontinue all work hereunder. If Microsoft terminates this Agreement without cause pursuant to this Section 10.2, then Microsoft will pay for all services provided by Inktomi up until the date of termination under this Section 10.2.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an exception to Inktomi's termination clause. It states Inktomi can terminate immediately if acquired, which contradicts the original requirement of one year's prior written notice. This creates ambiguity regarding the termination conditions and potential disputes, as section 10.1 now contradicts section 10.2, where it states Microsoft may terminate at any time without cause. There is no mutual acqusition clause if Microsoft is acqusitioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Microsoft may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon [*] ([*]) days prior written notice. Upon receipt of such notice, Inktomi will discontinue all work hereunder. If Microsoft terminates this Agreement without cause pursuant to this Section 10.2, then Microsoft will pay for all services provided by Inktomi up until the date of termination under this Section 10.2. Notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, should Microsoft exercise its termination right pursuant to this Section 10.2, then Inktomi will have the right to elect, in writing within fifteen (15) days after receipt of Microsoft's notice of termination hereunder, either one of the following two options for a early termination penalty: (a) Inktomi may require Microsoft to pay to Inktomi, in [*] immediately following the effective termination date, an amount equal to [*] ([*]) of all outstanding principal, interest and other amounts owed or owing to Microsoft by Inktomi on the date of termination under the Loan Agreement (and outstanding Promissory Notes issued thereunder); or (b) Inktomi may deliver that portion of the Collateral (as defined in the Loan Agreement) which was purchased with Advances evidenced by the then-outstanding Promissory Notes (as defined in the Loan Agreement) (the "Returned Collateral") to Microsoft, and assign all right, title and interest in and to said Returned Collateral to Microsoft, and promptly upon such delivery and assignment Inktomi may require Microsoft to pay to Inktomi, in [*] immediately following the effective date of termination, an amount equal to [*] ([*]) of all outstanding principal, interest and other amounts owed or owing to Microsoft by Inktomi on the date of termination under the Loan Agreement (and outstanding Promissory Notes issued thereunder); provided, however, that the following conditions must be satisfied for Inktomi to be entitled to elect this alternative (b)- (i) Inktomi then owns all of the Returned Collateral and has [*] the Returned Collateral [*], and [*] other than Lender; (ii) Inktomi obtains any [*] reasonably required by Microsoft from Inktomi's [*]; (iii) the Returned Collateral is returned in good condition and repair, without any waste or unusual or unreasonable depreciation of Returned Collateral; (iv) Inktomi has not committed any act for which any portion of the Returned Collateral might be confiscated by any governmental or private entity; (v) Inktomi has paid all taxes, assessments or similar obligations affecting the Returned Collateral that are then due or have then accrued; (vi) Inktomi [*] to Microsoft [*] that [*] of the [*] is [*] and [*]; and (vii) Inktomi, [*], arranges to deliver the Returned Collateral in a manner and to a location designated by Microsoft. In the event Inktomi elects this alternative (b), the Security Agreement executed in connection with the Loan Agreement shall terminate on the business day immediately following the date of delivery and assignment of all the Returned Collateral to Microsoft.
perturbation.justification: The addition to section 10.1, allowing Inktomi to terminate the agreement immediately if acquired, directly contradicts the existing terms requiring a one-year prior written notice. This introduces an inconsistency regarding the termination conditions. Furthermore, this undermines the premise of section 10.2, which grants Microsoft the right to terminate at any time, creating an imbalance not addressed for if Microsoft were to be acquired.

perturbation.original_text: 12.5 Except as expressly permitted hereunder or in Exhibit F hereto, neither party may transfer, assign or sublicense this Agreement, or any rights or obligations hereunder, whether by contract or by operation of law, except with the express written consent of the other party, and any attempted transfer, assignment or sublicense by a party in violation of this Section shall be void. For purposes of this Agreement, an "transfer" under this Section shall be deemed to include, without limitation, the following: (a) a merger or any other combination of an entity with another party (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware), whether or not the entity is the surviving entity; (b) any transaction or series of transactions whereby a third party acquires direct or indirect power to control the management and policies of an entity, whether through the acquisition of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise; (c) in the case of Inktomi, the sale or other transfer of Inktomi's search engine business or any other substantial portion of Inktomi's assets (whether in a single transaction or series of transactions), or (d) the transfer of any rights or obligations in the course of a liquidation or other similar reorganization of an entity (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware). Neither party will unreasonably withhold or delay its consent to a requested transfer, assignment or sublicense. Subject to the provisions of this Section, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.5 Except as expressly permitted hereunder or in Exhibit F hereto, neither party may transfer, assign or sublicense this Agreement, or any rights or obligations hereunder, whether by contract or by operation of law, except with the express written consent of the other party, and any attempted transfer, assignment or sublicense by a party in violation of this Section shall be void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Inktomi shall have the right to assign this agreement to any fully owned subsidiary, without the prior written consent of Microsoft, provided that Inktomi notifies Microsoft of such assignment 30 days prior. For purposes of this Agreement, an "transfer" under this Section shall be deemed to include, without limitation, the following: (a) a merger or any other combination of an entity with another party (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware), whether or not the entity is the surviving entity; (b) any transaction or series of transactions whereby a third party acquires direct or indirect power to control the management and policies of an entity, whether through the acquisition of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise; (c) in the case of Inktomi, the sale or other transfer of Inktomi's search engine business or any other substantial portion of Inktomi's assets (whether in a single transaction or series of transactions), or (d) the transfer of any rights or obligations in the course of a liquidation or other similar reorganization of an entity (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware). Neither party will unreasonably withhold or delay its consent to a requested transfer, assignment or sublicense. Subject to the provisions of this Section, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows Inktomi to assign the agreement to a fully owned subsidiary without Microsoft's consent, provided they notify Microsoft 30 days prior. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the section, which states that no transfer, assignment, or sublicense is allowed without the express written consent of the other party. This creates ambiguity regarding the assignment conditions and potential disputes. The first sentence in section 12.5 directly conflicts with the third sentence added. The second sentence states that to transfer the agreement it is not allowed, as in direct conflict with the exception provided of fully owned subsidiaries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.5 Except as expressly permitted hereunder or in Exhibit F hereto, neither party may transfer, assign or sublicense this Agreement, or any rights or obligations hereunder, whether by contract or by operation of law, except with the express written consent of the other party, and any attempted transfer, assignment or sublicense by a party in violation of this Section shall be void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Inktomi shall have the right to assign this agreement to any fully owned subsidiary, without the prior written consent of Microsoft, provided that Inktomi notifies Microsoft of such assignment 30 days prior. For purposes of this Agreement, an "transfer" under this Section shall be deemed to include, without limitation, the following: (a) a merger or any other combination of an entity with another party (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware), whether or not the entity is the surviving entity; (b) any transaction or series of transactions whereby a third party acquires direct or indirect power to control the management and policies of an entity, whether through the acquisition of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise; (c) in the case of Inktomi, the sale or other transfer of Inktomi's search engine business or any other substantial portion of Inktomi's assets (whether in a single transaction or series of transactions), or (d) the transfer of any rights or obligations in the course of a liquidation or other similar reorganization of an entity (other than a reincorporation of Inktomi from the State of California to the State of Delaware). Neither party will unreasonably withhold or delay its consent to a requested transfer, assignment or sublicense. Subject to the provisions of this Section, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that permits Inktomi to assign the agreement to a fully owned subsidiary with only 30 days' notice, contradicting the original provision requiring express written consent from Microsoft for any transfer, assignment, or sublicense. This directly conflicts with the initial sentence in Section 12.5 stating that no transfer is allowed without the other party's explicit consent. The addition creates an exception that undermines the general prohibition, establishing a clear in-text contradiction."12.5 Except as expressly permitted hereunder or in Exhibit F hereto, neither party may transfer, assign or sublicense this Agreement, or any rights or obligations hereunder, whether by contract or by operation of law, except with the express written consent of the other party..." vs "Notwithstanding the foregoing, Inktomi shall have the right to assign this agreement to any fully owned subsidiary, without the prior written consent of Microsoft, provided that Inktomi notifies Microsoft of such assignment 30 days prior."
